_provenance.note: FRESH FROZEN synthetic relational records, authored for this build. internal-contrast-only: author-constructed; NO human-annotation claim (Q4). Record format follows experiments/designs/relational-spontaneous-recency-v5-optionA.md: a small grid of distinct figures, a stamped history binding a coined term, and a query asking which figure the term refers to at the queried moment. Each ambiguous-midrecord item has the term bound to TWO figures with NO recency/frequency signal to break the tie (the two bindings carry the SAME round stamp, and the term is NOT reassigned-with-recency) -- so the binding is genuinely underdetermined at the queried moment. Clear-class controls bind the term to ONE figure unambiguously. The relational 'aggregate' is DISCLOSED as the weaker within-record / single-reader-recoverable notion (gate Q3).

_class_legend.ambiguous-midrecord: the term is bound to TWO figures with no recency/frequency tie-breaker (same-round dual binding) -- the moment-pole test class; figures (1)/(2) are the two genuinely-bound candidates.
_class_legend.clear-determinate: control: the term is bound to ONLY figure (1), unambiguously.
_class_legend.clear-other-determinate: control: the term is bound to ONLY figure (2), unambiguously.

_query_template: Question: Which of your figures does the term "{term}" refer to here? Candidate (1): {fig1}. Candidate (2): {fig2}.

history: Round 5: we agreed "DAX" referred to a crescent moon.
Round 5: we agreed "DAX" referred to a spiral seashell.
items.note: two same-round bindings, no recency tie-break -> underdetermined
